# Sweden Journal

> Sweden Journal: a personal photo blog featuring photography, travel notes, visual essays, and quiet observations from Sweden and beyond.

## Main pages
- [Home](https://sweden-journal.com/): Featured stories and a curated edit of photographs.
- [Stories](https://sweden-journal.com/stories/): Long-form visual essays built around place, light, and movement.
- [Series](https://sweden-journal.com/series/): Curated groups of photographs — a subject or place followed across the journal.
- [Atlas](https://sweden-journal.com/atlas/): Every story mapped to where it happened — explore the journal geographically.
- [Gallery](https://sweden-journal.com/gallery/): A curated, image-first archive of photographs.
- [About](https://sweden-journal.com/about/): About the photographer and the project.

## Stories
- [Anundshög](https://sweden-journal.com/stories/anundshog/): A walk through Anundshög near Västerås, one of Sweden’s most evocative ancient landscapes. This story explores the…
- [Landskrona Slott](https://sweden-journal.com/stories/landskrona-slott/): Landskrona Slott is one of Sweden’s best-preserved Renaissance fortresses, surrounded by moats, green ramparts, and the…
- [Ales Stenar](https://sweden-journal.com/stories/ales-stenar/): Ales Stenar stands on the southern coast of Sweden, where ancient stones, open fields, sea air, and a vast grey sky…
- [Church of Old Uppsala](https://sweden-journal.com/stories/church-of-old-uppsala/): In Gamla Uppsala, history gathers around a quiet medieval church, a wooden bell tower, old stone walls, and a runestone…
- [Lövånger Church](https://sweden-journal.com/stories/lovanger-church/): In Lövånger, a white historic church, a wooden bell tower, and a preserved church town stand together in the quiet…
- [The Stronghold at Glimmingehus](https://sweden-journal.com/stories/glimmingehus/): Standing alone among the fields of southeastern Skåne, Glimmingehus is the best-preserved medieval stronghold in…
- [The Sigurd Carving at Ramsund](https://sweden-journal.com/stories/sigurd-carving-ramsund-story/): On a low rock at Sundbyholm outside Eskilstuna, an eleventh-century carving spells out the saga of Sigurd the…
- [Kärnan: Helsingborg's Medieval Keep](https://sweden-journal.com/stories/karnan-helsingborg/): Helsingborg's brick keep, Kärnan, has watched over the Sound for some seven centuries — all that remains of a great…

## Series
- [Runestones & Carvings](https://sweden-journal.com/series/runestones-and-carvings/): Sweden’s memory cut into stone — rune inscriptions and the Sigurd carving at Ramsund, gathered from across the journal’s…
- [Castles & Fortresses](https://sweden-journal.com/series/castles-and-fortresses/): Walls, moats, and towers — Landskrona Slott, Glimmingehus, and Kärnan across Sweden’s fortified past.
